overview: Marine Iguana (Amblyrhynchus cristatus): Endemic to the Galápagos Islands, these are the world's only ocean-dwelling lizards. Unique Salt Glands: They possess special glands to filter salt from their blood, which they then expel by "sneezing" it out through their noses. Dietary Adaptations: They have blunt noses and sharp teeth specifically adapted to scrape algae off underwater rocks. Size Reduction: During harsh climate events like El Niño, they can shrink in size by up to 20% to survive food shortages. Smithsonian Magazine +5 6 sites Behold: The Galápagos' Marine Iguana - Smithsonian Magazine
